mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods following a FEDFUNDS increase, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household consumption and corporate profit margins, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observations when UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, triggering negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction that lowers near-term earnings expectations, producing negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the signal shows no predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, leading to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1400 coincide with positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total employment and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yield negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent, cheap financing conditions stimulate risk appetite and lower corporate borrowing costs, producing positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS below 2.0 percent is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial output and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent increases in INDPRO and GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day returns in over 50% of cases.
